text.title: Side-Project Financial Runway Examples
text.summary: Worked examples — one stay-and-prepare verdict, one too-early leave that the trigger blocks.

example.title: Stay-and-prepare with geo-arbitrage runway-extender
example.note: burn_personal $6000 + burn_business $1000 = $7000/mo. savings $90000. mrr $2500, 3mo growth 12%. runway = 90000 / (7000 - 2500) = 20 months. mrr_pct_of_burn = 2500/7000 = 0.357. growth = +12%. All three trigger conditions met (runway >= 12, mrr_pct >= 0.30, growth > 0). Geo-arbitrage scenario: Lisbon, burn drops 45% → new burn $3850. Runway extends to ~28mo even at flat MRR. Stress tests: flat MRR 12mo → 12.8mo; -20% MRR over 6mo → 11.2mo (under the 12-month floor). Verdict: decision_status=stay-and-prepare. Operator continues day job 1-2 quarters; if MRR holds + growth continues, next quarter switches to leave. Validator: PASS.

example.title: 6-month leave-job trigger the methodology rejects
example.note: burn $5000 (forgot health insurance + retirement). savings $20000. mrr $1000. runway = 20000/4000 = 5 months. mrr_pct_of_burn = 0.20. growth = -5%. Operator proposes decision_status=leave because "burn is light and I'll grow MRR fast". Validator BLOCKS: f1 (burn excludes health), f2 (runway < 12 AND mrr_pct < 0.30), f3 (stress_tests empty). Repair: recompute burn with all categories ($7500), stay on day job, model both stress tests, defer decision to next quarter.
